vBRIEFInfo.description: Story vBRIEF for #1595 PR4 MAP projection and codebase-map freshness check.

plan.title: Generate codebase MAP projection and freshness check

narratives.Description: PR4 for #1595 renders a generated codebase MAP from the PR3 codebase-map artifact contract. The codebase:map path consumes a conformant provider artifact at a configured path when present and valid, otherwise degrades to the deterministic default extractor. The companion freshness check validates generated projection drift without making provider invocation part of canonical project data.
narratives.ImplementationPlan: 1. Add a renderer that converts a validated codebase-map.v1 artifact into a banner-marked .planning/codebase/MAP.md orientation document. 2. Extend provider selection to support durable artifact-at-a-path consumption from plan.policy.projectionProviders["codebase-map"], including expectation validation and default fallback on absence, invalidity, mismatch, or staleness. 3. Add codebase:map and verify:codebase-map-fresh task surfaces that remain invocation-agnostic and do not store task/deft command strings in canonical data. 4. Implement a no-network freshness protocol based on validated artifact provenance and content hashes, with clear fallback behavior. 5. Add focused tests, docs, and CHANGELOG coverage without adding a heavy crawl/summarize engine or consumer propagation.
narratives.UserStory: As a maintainer, I want a generated MAP projection from vBRIEF-owned codeStructure metadata plus provider/default-derived facts, so agents can orient quickly while vBRIEF remains the canonical source of truth.
narratives.NonGoals: No heavy in-directive crawl or summarize engine, no model or network dependency, no standardized provider CLI ABI, no optional source-header projection, no consumer AGENTS/template propagation, and no command strings stored in projectionManifest or provider policy.

items.title: Generated MAP renderer emits a banner-marked .planning/codebase/MAP.md from a conformant codebase-map.v1 artifact.
Provider artifact-at-a-path policy is read as a validation assertion and falls back to the default extractor when absent, invalid, mismatched, or stale.
codebase:map and verify:codebase-map-fresh task surfaces are wired without storing command strings in canonical metadata.
Freshness checks compare generated projection inputs against canonical/derived provenance and never reverse drift direction.
Tests, docs, and CHANGELOG cover PR4 behavior and preserve the no-heavy-engine boundary.

references.title: Issue #1595: feat(codebase): generate codebase MAP from vBRIEF-owned codeStructure metadata
PR #1680: feat(codebase): add provider contract layer
Issue #1710: Engine code-freeze for the Python to TypeScript migration
Issue #1530: RFC: Migrate directive's enforcement engine from Python to TypeScript/Node.js

x-tracking.freeze_override: Operator explicitly approved cleanup then proceed during #1710 engine freeze on 2026-06-18.

verify_commands: uv run pytest tests/cli/test_codebase_map.py tests/cli/test_codebase_map_fresh.py tests/cli/test_codebase_provider.py -q
expected_outputs: Generated MAP exists as a projection, not canonical metadata
Provider artifact policy is validation-only and command-free
Missing or invalid provider artifacts gracefully fall back to the default extractor
Freshness check detects projection drift without using network or model calls
